reason: Reconciliation bill framework with no summary provided—highest risk signal. Reconciliation is the primary vector for entitlement cuts, tax expenditure reshuffling, and deficit-accounting gamesmanship. The absence of summary text itself suggests complex payload requiring full kernel extraction.
vectors: fiscal bleed (reconciliation allows bypass of normal deficit rules)
regulatory capture (carve-outs and safe harbors standard in reconciliation riders)
title: An act to provide for reconciliation pursuant to title II of H. Con. Res. 14.

reason: Rescissions bills are direct fiscal instruments that claw back appropriated funds—classic deficit-reduction theater that often protects favored programs while gutting vulnerable populations. Without summary text, the kernel mechanics are opaque, requiring immediate full audit to identify which agencies absorb cuts and which escape.
vectors: Fiscal bleed: selective rescission patterns can redirect budget authority away from oversight agencies toward defense/DHS
Entitlement erosion: rescissions frequently target Medicaid, SNAP, HUD line-items buried in appropriations
Regulatory capture: rescissions can defund enforcement (EPA, FTC, Labor) while protecting subsidy streams for positioned industries
Procedural exploit: rescission authority under AARA allows executive de facto line-item veto—requires mapping of which programs targeted vs. protected
title: Rescissions Act of 2025
